reasoning: There is currently no vaccine for Chlamydophila pneumoniae, and recent research (2023) confirms that candidates are only in the early pre-clinical or in-silico design stages. Moreover, historical trials for this pathogen have failed. Developing a vaccine from in-silico to clinical availability takes roughly 5 to 10+ years. Therefore, it is practically impossible that a vaccine will be developed and appropriately cited on the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' page by any of the 2025 resolution dates. My probabilities are at or near the 0.05 minimum boundary permitted by the instructions, with a tiny upward drift over time to account for long-tail risks, such as vandalism or a user prematurely adding an experimental vaccine to the list.
